commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and merge decision for the focused candidate fix.; Cleared: The diff changes Android onboarding permission UI and unit tests only; it adds no dependencies, workflows, package execution, secret handling, or supply-chain surface. (sha=4e9ef75fccddbcfd283ffbc925ac27912f51c07e)
commands.review_summary:
Updates Android onboarding QR scan failure handling to show camera-specific recovery copy, request CAMERA after a denied scanner failure, add an app-settings action, and cover the logic with unit tests.

PR surface: Other +143. Total +143 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main maps QR scanner startup failure to generic copy while Android already declares and tracks CAMERA permission state. The contributor also posted an exact-head emulator flow for the denied-camera path, but I did not independently run an emulator in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and merge decision for the focused candidate fix.

target.title: fix(android): explain camera permission for setup scans
target.body:
Closes #98044.

### What Problem This Solves
When Android setup QR scanning failed because Camera permission was denied, onboarding only showed generic scanner failure copy and did not guide users to grant the permission.

### Evidence
At PR head `4e9ef75fcc`, focused Play and third-party unit tests pass for `OnboardingFlowLogicTest`. They cover camera-specific scanner copy, the app-settings recovery decision, non-camera setup errors hiding the camera settings action, and the scan-time CAMERA grant updating the final onboarding permission state.

Manual UI validation:
On `sdk_gphone64_arm64`, Android 16 / API 36, using the Play debug APK (`versionName=2026.6.10`, `targetSdk=36`), Camera was revoked for OpenClaw and Google Play services. `Connect Gateway` -> `Scan setup code` first showed Google scanner `Camera permission required`; after dismissing it, Android requested `Allow OpenClaw Node to take pictures and record video?`; after `Don’t allow`, Gateway Setup showed `Camera permission is required to scan setup codes. Open Android app settings to grant Camera permission, then try again.` with the `Open app settings` action. A follow-up UI dump after leaving the camera-specific state no longer contained `Open app settings`.
target.author: turbotheturtle

### What Problem This Solves

### Evidence
At PR head `4e9ef75fcc`, focused Play and third-party unit tests pass for `OnboardingFlowLogicTest`. They cover camera-specific scanner copy, the app-settings recovery decision, non-camera setup errors hiding the camera settings action, and the scan-time CAMERA grant updating the final onboarding permission state.

Manual UI validation:
On `sdk_gphone64_arm64`, Android 16 / API 36, using the Play debug APK (`versionName=2026.6.10`, `targetSdk=36`), Camera was revoked for OpenClaw and Google Play services. `Connect Gateway` -> `Scan setup code` first showed Google scanner `Camera permission required`; after dismissing it, Android requested `Allow OpenClaw Node to take pictures and record video?`; after `Don’t allow`, Gateway Setup showed `Camera permission is required to scan setup codes. Open Android app settings to grant Camera permission, then try again.` with the `Open app settings` action. A follow-up UI dump after leaving the camera-specific state no longer contained `Open app settings`.
